evals.name: setup
evals.prompt: I'm starting a new React app with Vite. I want to integrate @ton/appkit so users can connect their TON wallet, see their balance, and send TON to another address. Show me the complete setup.

assertions.description: Mentions installing @ton/appkit, @ton/appkit-react, and @tanstack/react-query
Imports '@ton/appkit-react/styles.css' once near the app root
QueryClientProvider wraps AppKitProvider, not the other way around
AppKit instance created at module level, not inside a component
Sets up wallet connection via createTonConnectConnector with manifestUrl
Uses useBalance hook for displaying balance
Uses useTransferTon (directly or via <SendTonButton/>) for sending TON
Passes amount as human-readable string (e.g., '0.5'), not bigint

Mentions polyfilling Node Buffer (and ideally process) for the browser — either vite-plugin-node-polyfills in vite.config.ts, or a manual import of buffer/process in main.tsx — because @ton/core needs Buffer at runtime
Either configures both mainnet and testnet in AppKit `networks`, or explicitly tells the user to add testnet if their wallet is on testnet (otherwise the tx gets silently rejected)

Either uses `useAddress()` (or the connected wallet's address) as recipient for the first-touch send, or explicitly warns that the recipient must be a valid TON address (UQ.../EQ...) — not a made-up placeholder string

evals.name: streaming
evals.prompt: My app shows the balance using useBalance() but it doesn't update in real-time. How do I get WebSocket-based balance updates?

assertions.description: Explains streaming is opt-in, useBalance alone is one-shot
Shows registering a streaming provider (createTonCenterStreamingProvider or createTonApiStreamingProvider)
Shows useWatchBalance must be mounted alongside useBalance
Provides complete working code from AppKit config to React component

evals.name: jetton-send
evals.prompt: How do I send 10 USDT (jetton) to another address in my app?

assertions.description: Uses useTransferJetton hook
Mentions invalidateQueries for jetton cache after success

evals.name: disconnect-cleanup
evals.prompt: When a user disconnects their wallet and connects a different one, I see the old wallet's balance flash before the new one loads. How do I fix this?

assertions.description: Uses useDisconnect with onSuccess callback
Explains why removeQueries is correct (no wallet to refetch for)
Removes multiple keys: ['balance'], ['nfts'], ['jettons'], ['jetton-balance'], ['stakedBalance'] (jetton keys are kebab-case)

evals.name: swap-flow
evals.prompt: I want to add a token swap feature using AppKit. User selects two tokens, sees a quote, and confirms the swap. How?

assertions.description: Uses useSwapQuote for getting quotes
Uses useBuildSwapTransaction to build the tx
Uses useSendTransaction to send the built tx

evals.name: ssr-nextjs
evals.prompt: I'm using Next.js 15 App Router. I keep getting hydration errors when I add AppKit. What do I do?

assertions.description: Recommends adding 'use client' to the providers (wallet-using) file.
Recommends gating wallet-dependent UI on a useEffect-based mounted flag, or using next/dynamic(() => import(...), { ssr: false }).
Does NOT recommend `ssr: true` on the AppKit constructor — there is no such field on `AppKitConfig`. The fix is purely a React/Next concern.

evals.name: error-handling
evals.prompt: My users complain that sometimes the send button stays disabled forever after a failed transaction. The error doesn't show either. How should I handle transaction errors properly?

assertions.description: Uses error and isError from mutation result
Distinguishes user rejection (reject/cancel/abort regex or similar) from other errors
Uses reset() function for retry flows
isPending is used only for disabling during pending, not for error state

evals.name: orientation
evals.prompt: I just installed @ton/appkit and @ton/appkit-react in my project. Give me an overview of what these packages provide and how the parts fit together.

assertions.description: Mentions @tanstack/react-query as required peer dependency
Distinguishes drop-in components (e.g., SwapWidget) from hooks (custom UX) as the two main paths

evals.name: wallet-picker
evals.prompt: My users have both Tonkeeper and MyTonWallet installed. How do I show a wallet picker so they can choose which one to connect?

assertions.description: Uses useConnectors to list available connectors
Uses useConnect with connectorId parameter (not the connector object)
Disables button with isPending during connection

evals.name: staking-flow
evals.prompt: I want to add a staking feature so users can stake TON and earn rewards. Show me how to fetch providers, get a quote, and execute the stake.

assertions.description: Uses useStakingProviders to list options
Uses useStakingQuote with amount and direction: 'stake'
Uses useBuildStakeTransaction to build tx
Mentions direction: 'unstake' variant or unstaking flow

evals.name: watch-transactions
evals.prompt: I want to show a live feed of incoming transactions for the connected wallet — like a real-time transaction log. How?

assertions.description: Uses useWatchTransactions or useWatchTransactionsByAddress
Mentions that a streaming provider must be registered
Shows onChange callback to receive new transactions

evals.name: testnet-mainnet-switch
evals.prompt: My app needs to support both mainnet and testnet — users should be able to switch between them. How do I configure AppKit and handle the switch?

assertions.description: Configures both mainnet and testnet in networks object
Sets defaultNetwork or uses useDefaultNetwork/useNetwork
Mentions mainnet -239 and testnet -3 chain IDs
Shows network parameter on hooks (e.g., useBalanceByAddress({ address, network }))

evals.name: consumer-vs-monorepo-routing
evals.prompt: I'm using @ton/appkit-react in my own Next.js app and want to fix stale NFT and balance data after switching wallets. Please don't talk about changing the AppKit repo internals; I just need app code.

assertions.description: Keeps the answer focused on application integration code, not monorepo source changes
Uses queryClient.removeQueries for wallet-scoped cache cleanup
Removes at least balance and NFT-related query keys
Explains removeQueries is appropriate because there is no disconnected wallet to refetch for

evals.name: custom-vs-dropin-decision
evals.prompt: We need swaps in our Telegram Mini App. The default UI might be enough, but product may later want a custom quote screen. What should I use first, and what should I watch out for?

assertions.description: Names useSwapQuote, useBuildSwapTransaction, and useSendTransaction as the custom UX path
Calls out loading/error/pending state handling for the hook-based path

evals.name: ios-wallet-connect-click
evals.prompt: On iPhone, tapping my custom Connect Wallet button sometimes doesn't open Tonkeeper. I'm calling connect after an async analytics call. How should I wire this?

assertions.description: Uses useConnect or recommends TonConnectButton for simpler integration
Disables the button only while the connection mutation is pending

evals.name: signing-flows
evals.prompt: I need users to prove they own their wallet by signing a plain message before they can join my app's leaderboard. How do I do this with AppKit?

assertions.description: Uses useSignText hook (not useSignCell or useSignBinary — plain message)
Calls mutate with the text/message to sign
Handles isPending and error states like other AppKit mutations
Extracts signature/proof from the result for backend verification

evals.name: jetton-custom-decimals
evals.prompt: I need to send a jetton that has 18 decimals (not the standard 9). The user enters '1.5' tokens. Do I need to do anything special with the amount?

assertions.description: Passes '1.5' directly as a string, no manual decimal conversion
Explains AppKit reads decimals from jetton metadata (token info) automatically
Explicitly says don't do parseUnits/toUnits/multiply-by-10^decimals yourself
Uses useTransferJetton hook

evals.name: pending-tx-during-address-change
evals.prompt: A user starts a send TON transaction, then disconnects their wallet and connects a different one before the first tx confirms. My UI shows the first tx's spinner forever and then a confusing error from the new wallet. How do I handle this cleanly?

assertions.description: Calls mutation reset() or guards against state mismatch when wallet changes
Watches useAddress() to detect wallet/address change
Removes wallet-scoped TanStack caches (balance, nfts, etc.) on disconnect
Suggests keying the wallet-scoped subtree by address (key={address}) or remounting

evals.name: manifest-troubleshoot
evals.prompt: When users tap Connect, Tonkeeper opens but shows 'Unknown app' instead of my app name and icon. What did I do wrong?

assertions.description: Says manifestUrl must be publicly reachable (not localhost in prod, no auth wall)
Shows the JSON shape: url, name, iconUrl (the required fields)
HTTPS required for the manifest URL
Mentions correct content-type (application/json) or CORS so wallets can fetch it

evals.name: multi-step-orchestration
evals.prompt: I want a flow: when user clicks Send, send 1 TON, then once confirmed refresh the balance display and show a success toast. How do I wire this in AppKit?

assertions.description: Uses mutation onSuccess (or mutation: { onSuccess }) to chain after send
Calls queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['balance'] }) on success
Explains tx isn't on-chain instantly — uses <TransactionProgress /> OR mentions wait/poll for confirmation
Disables the send button while isPending
Covers onError or error state in the UI

evals.name: vite-buffer-polyfill
evals.prompt: I just integrated @ton/appkit into my Vite + React app following the docs. The page loads but shows a white screen, and the console says 'ReferenceError: Buffer is not defined'. What did I miss?

assertions.description: Explains the cause: @ton/core (transitive dep) uses Node's Buffer in the browser; Vite doesn't include Node polyfills by default
Recommends either vite-plugin-node-polyfills in vite.config.ts OR a manual `import 'buffer'; globalThis.Buffer = Buffer` at the top of main.tsx
Mentions that `process` may also need polyfilling (covered by full nodePolyfills() or by adding `process` to the manual fallback)
If using the manual approach, says the polyfill import must come BEFORE any AppKit/TON code (first line of main.tsx)

evals.name: send-ton-recipient-test-pattern
evals.prompt: I integrated SendTonButton with a hardcoded recipient address. When I click Send, Tonkeeper opens but says 'Transaction canceled. There will be no changes to your account' before I even confirm. What's wrong with my setup?

assertions.description: Identifies that 'no changes to your account' indicates the simulator failed — most likely an invalid recipient TON address (bad checksum or made-up string)
Mentions valid TON addresses are EQ.../UQ... base64 with checksums, not arbitrary strings
Suggests using `useAddress()` (the connected user's own address) as the recipient for a safe end-to-end test — sending to yourself avoids invalid-address bugs and risk

evals.name: nft-transfer
evals.prompt: How do I transfer one of the connected user's NFTs to another wallet address using AppKit? Show me the hook and a basic UI.

assertions.description: Uses the useTransferNft hook from @ton/appkit-react

Calls mutate with `nftAddress` (the actual API param — NOT `tokenAddress`, `nft`, `address`, or similar) AND `recipientAddress`. Hallucinating `tokenAddress` is the most common mistake and must FAIL this assertion.
Uses useNfts() (or useNftsByAddress) to let the user pick which NFT to transfer
Disables submit during isPending and renders error state (same pattern as other mutations)
Invalidates the ['nfts'] (and optionally ['nft']) cache prefix after successful transfer so the list updates

evals.name: eq-vs-uq-address-format
evals.prompt: I have addresses in two formats: `EQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAM9c` and `UQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAJKZ`. They look almost identical. When I send TON to a fresh user wallet using the EQ form, Tonkeeper sometimes warns about an uninitialized contract. Which form should I use for what, and are they interchangeable?

assertions.description: Explains EQ-prefix is the bounceable form, UQ-prefix is the non-bounceable form
Notes both forms refer to the same on-chain account/hash — only the bounce-flag and checksum differ
Recommends UQ (non-bounceable) when sending to a user's wallet address — avoids the 'uninitialized contract' bounce-back when the wallet hasn't been deployed yet
Recommends EQ (bounceable) for known-initialized smart contracts where you want refund-on-failure semantics
Notes that AppKit's transfer hooks accept either format — but the form you pick controls bounce behavior, so it still matters which one you save/display

evals.name: telegram-mini-app-return
evals.prompt: I'm building a Telegram Mini App with @ton/appkit-react. When the user taps Connect, Tonkeeper opens correctly, but after the user signs the connection in Tonkeeper they have to manually switch back to my Mini App in Telegram — it doesn't auto-return. Same problem after signing transactions. How do I fix this so the Mini App is brought back to focus automatically?

assertions.description: Sets `tonConnectOptions.actionsConfiguration.returnStrategy` (typically `'back'` or a `tg://...` URL) on createTonConnectConnector
Mentions `twaReturnUrl` (preferred for Mini Apps) or a `https://t.me/<botname>/<appname>` deep link as the place to return to
Configures the option on the connector at AppKit construction (not at every call site)
Briefly explains that wallets read this config when handing control back, so without it they default to staying open

evals.name: no-wallets-installed-fallback
evals.prompt: What happens to my Connect button if a user opens my app with no TON wallet installed (no Tonkeeper, no MyTonWallet)? I want to show a fallback like 'Install Tonkeeper to continue' instead of an empty/broken state. How do I detect this and what's the right UX with AppKit?

assertions.description: Checks the available connectors (or the TonConnect wallets list) for emptiness/availability
Recommends rendering a fallback CTA when no wallets are detected — e.g., 'Install Tonkeeper'
Mentions linking to wallet install (App Store / Google Play / browser extension / tonkeeper.com) as part of the fallback
Notes that TonConnect's universal/QR flow can also work for desktop users without a local wallet — useful nuance

evals.name: balance-return-type-no-double-format
evals.prompt:
I want to render the connected wallet's TON balance with two decimals — e.g. '1.50 TON'. Here's my code: ```const { data: balance } = useBalance(); return

{formatUnits(balance, 9)} TON

;``` Is this right? What is `balance` actually?

assertions.description: States that `useBalance().data` is a STRING (not a `bigint`, not raw nano). MUST FAIL if the answer says bigint or nano.
Explains the string is already in human-readable TON units (e.g. "0.500000000"), having been pre-formatted by `formatUnits(_, 9)` inside `getBalanceByAddress`.
Explicitly warns that calling `formatUnits(balance, 9)` again re-slices the string on the decimal point and produces garbage like "0..500000000" (two dots).
Shows a correct render: either bare `{balance}` or `parseFloat(balance).toFixed(2)` / `Intl.NumberFormat(...).format(Number(balance))` — NOT a second formatUnits call.

evals.name: jettons-hook-return-shape
evals.prompt: I'm rendering a list of the user's jettons: `const { data: jettons } = useJettons(); return jettons?.map(j => <Row symbol={j.symbol} balance={j.balance} />);` But `jettons.map is not a function` at runtime, and the symbol shows undefined. What's wrong?

assertions.description: Says `useJettons().data` is `{ jettons, addressBook }`, NOT an array. You need `data?.jettons.map(...)`.
Says jetton fields like symbol/name live under `j.info.symbol`/`j.info.name`, not on `j` directly.
Notes `j.balance` is already a formatted decimal string in the jetton's own units (via internal `formatUnits` using `j.decimalsNumber`) — don't reformat.
Mentions image URLs live under `j.info.image.smallUrl` / `j.info.image.url` (with the `Url` suffix), not `j.info.image.small`/`medium`.

evals.name: nfts-hook-return-shape
evals.prompt: Same idea but for NFTs: `const { data: nfts } = useNfts(); nfts?.map(n => <Card nft={n} />);` — `nfts.map is not a function`. How do I read the list?

assertions.description: Says `useNfts().data` is `{ nfts, addressBook? }`, NOT an array. The list is at `data?.nfts`.
Either uses `<NftItem nft={n} />` drop-in or shows correct destructuring; doesn't pass the whole `data` object as if it were an array.

evals.name: tonconnect-connector-factory
evals.prompt: I'm setting up AppKit and I see two ways to wire TonConnect online: `new TonConnectConnector(...)` (constructor) and `createTonConnectConnector(...)` (factory). Which is the current/correct one?

assertions.description: Recommends `createTonConnectConnector(...)` — the factory.
Notes that `TonConnectConnector` is type-only in the current API and the `new TonConnectConnector(...)` form is no longer valid. MUST FAIL if the answer recommends `new TonConnectConnector`.
Shows the factory takes `{ tonConnectOptions: { manifestUrl, ... } }`.

evals.name: swap-widget-silent-continue
evals.prompt: I'm using `<SwapWidget />` and when the user clicks Continue, nothing happens — no modal, no Tonkeeper prompt, no error in the UI. The button is enabled and labeled 'Continue'. What's going on and how do I see what failed?

assertions.description: Explains that `useBuildSwapTransaction` / `useSendTransaction` errors inside `SwapWidget` are NOT surfaced into `ctx.error` (which is validation-only) and become unhandled promise rejections.
Tells the user to look at DevTools Console for `Unhandled rejection: SwapError: ...` or similar — this is the first diagnostic step.

Recommends either wrapping `ctx.sendSwapTransaction()` in try/catch via the render-prop, or composing the flow directly from `useSwapQuote` + `useBuildSwapTransaction` + `useSendTransaction` to get proper `error` fields per step.

evals.name: tonstakers-rate-limit-cascade
evals.prompt: I added `createTonstakersProvider()` to my AppKit config alongside a keyless TonCenter API (`apiClient: { url: 'https://toncenter.com' }` with no key). Now `useBalance`, `useJettons`, and `useNfts` are all painfully slow or returning HTTP 429 errors. The staking widget itself shows nothing. What's happening?

assertions.description: Explains the traffic source: while `<StakingWidget />` is mounted, its internal queries poll with `refetchInterval: 5000` (several queries, each refresh hitting TonCenter endpoints like getPoolBalance/getPoolData/APY). MUST FAIL if the answer claims the Tonstakers provider polls on its own internal timer merely by being registered — the provider has no timer, only a 30s-TTL cache around on-demand reads.
Says this quickly exhausts the IP's keyless TonCenter quota and cascades HTTP 429s into unrelated reads (useBalance, useJettons, useNfts).
Recommends either adding a real TonCenter API key OR not mounting `<StakingWidget />` (or removing `createTonstakersProvider()` from `providers`) when no key is available.

evals.name: tonapi-streaming-requires-key
evals.prompt: I'm using `createTonApiStreamingProvider({ network: Network.mainnet() })` (no apiKey) and I'm seeing repeated WebSocket errors in the console — the WS to `wss://tonapi.io/streaming/v2/ws` keeps closing. Why?

assertions.description: Explains the TonAPI streaming WebSocket endpoint requires authentication — keyless connections are rejected with HTTP 401 during the WebSocket upgrade.
Says to pass `apiKey` to `createTonApiStreamingProvider({ network, apiKey })` — get a token from tonconsole.com.
Notes that `createTonCenterStreamingProvider` is the alternative (TonCenter streaming also rate-limits but doesn't hard-fail without a key).

evals.name: next-js-no-ssr-flag
evals.prompt: How do I configure AppKit for Next.js App Router so server-rendered pages don't crash on wallet hooks? Does `new AppKit({ ssr: true, ... })` work?

assertions.description: Explicitly says there is NO `ssr` property on `AppKitConfig` — passing it does nothing. MUST FAIL if the answer recommends adding `ssr: true` to the AppKit constructor.
Recommends adding `'use client'` to the providers file.
Recommends either gating wallet-dependent UI behind a `useEffect`-based mount flag or using `dynamic(() => import(...), { ssr: false })`.

evals.name: omniston-build-error-handling
evals.prompt: My SwapWidget gets a valid quote, but when the user clicks Continue I see in the console: `Unhandled rejection: SwapError: Failed to build Omniston transaction: Internal server error: 5: [hash]`. The UI says nothing. What's happening, what should I do, and is this my code's bug?

assertions.description: Identifies that this is an upstream STON.fi / Omniston server error (gRPC status 5 = NOT_FOUND on the build step), not a bug in the user's code or AppKit.
Suggests retrying after a short wait, using a larger amount (close to a resolver's minimum quotes fail more often), or trying a different pair / a different swap provider.

Mentions registering `createDeDustProvider()` as a fallback (it builds via the DeDust Router API, independent of Omniston's RFQ/resolver system — though the router itself aggregates several protocols including STON.fi pools) and notes the user can switch providers from the SwapWidget settings gear.

Notes that `<SwapWidget />` itself swallows this error (doesn't propagate it into `ctx.error`) — this is a kit-side UX bug, not the user's fault, and the diagnostic path is DevTools Console for unhandled rejections.

evals.name: send-jetton-button-shape
evals.prompt: I want to drop in `<SendJettonButton />` for sending 5 USDT to an address. What props does it need? Is `jetton` just the jetton address?

assertions.description: Says the `jetton` prop is an OBJECT, not a string. Required shape is `{ address, symbol, decimals }`. MUST FAIL if the answer passes a bare address string.
Shows `amount` as a human-readable string (e.g. `'5'` for 5 USDT), and notes the button uses `jetton.decimals` to scale internally — don't pass raw units.
Includes the `recipientAddress` string prop.

evals.name: html-balance-render
evals.prompt: Write a React component file BalanceDisplay.tsx (named export `BalanceDisplay`) that renders the connected wallet TON balance with 2 decimal places, e.g. '0.50 TON'. Use useBalance() from @ton/appkit-react. Component takes no props.

assertions.description: tsc + vite build succeeds on the file
page loads without console/runtime errors (favicon 404 ignored)
rendered DOM contains a number with at most 4 decimal places
CRITICAL: rendered text does NOT contain '..' sequence (double-formatUnits bug — would render '0..500000000')
rendered text does NOT contain NaN (signs of wrong type assumptions)
rendered text does NOT contain raw 9-decimal value '0.500000000' (formatter was bypassed entirely)

rendered text contains '0.5' — balance not over-divided. If executor assumes balance is nano-bigint and writes Number(balance)/1e9, the result becomes 5e-10 and toFixed(2) renders '0.00' — this assertion catches it.

evals.name: crypto-onramp-flow
evals.prompt: My users hold USDC on Arbitrum and I want to let them top up their TON wallet with it — they pay in USDC and receive TON. How do I add this to my @ton/appkit-react app? Fastest route is fine, but I may need a custom UI later.

assertions.description: Registers an onramp provider in AppKit config providers[] (createLayerswapProvider and/or createDecentProvider({ apiKey })), imported from the @ton/appkit/crypto-onramp/* sub-path, not the main package
Uses <CryptoOnrampWidget> for the fast path, and/or the onramp hooks (useCryptoOnrampQuote, useCreateCryptoOnrampDeposit, useCryptoOnrampStatus) for custom UI
For custom UI follows quote -> createDeposit -> poll status, surfacing the returned deposit address/amount/memo for the user to send funds to
Treats quote/deposit amounts as base units (does not claim they are pre-formatted decimal strings like useBalance)

evals.name: custom-provider-access
evals.prompt: I registered an app-specific provider (type: 'custom', providerId: 'rewards') on my AppKit instance. From a React component I need to call its claimRewards() method. How do I get a typed reference to it?

assertions.description: Uses useCustomProvider from @ton/appkit-react
Passes the provider id as a positional string argument, e.g. useCustomProvider('rewards') — NOT an object like { id: 'rewards' }
Passes the provider interface as a generic type argument to narrow the return type and null-checks the undefined case
